A proposal · May 2026

You built something scholars love. Now let's make sure they find it.

Wonders is already trusted by researchers at Cambridge, Michigan, Brown, Dartmouth and Imperial — 550M+ sources across 88,000+ journals at their fingertips. Credibility, earned. What's left is the operator who ships content week after week, turns that institutional trust into reach, and measures every step. That's the role I'd fill — and what you see below is a starting-point sketch we'd refine together.

01 · The opportunity

The hard part is done.
The reach part is the lever.

Wonders has earned the kind of credibility most startups spend years chasing — universities trust the product, the catalog is enormous, the use case is real. There's only one number that hasn't caught up yet.

What you've built
550M+

sources across 88,000+ journals — and a roster of Cambridge, Michigan, Brown, Dartmouth and Imperial. Enterprise-grade trust, already earned.

vs.
Where there's room · three channels, three jobs
~265

LinkedIn followers — and a similar starting line on Instagram & TikTok. Three channels, three audiences: visibility for students & early researchers, credibility for decision-makers. Not a flaw — the lever.

Building the tool researchers trust is the hard part. Carrying it to its audience is the next chapter — and the part I do.

02 · What I'm seeing

Six quiet levers
between the product and its audience.

A first-pass read from the outside, before we've ever spoken. These aren't problems — they're levers.

01

The trust signals are buried below the fold.

"Cambridge · Michigan · Brown · Dartmouth · Imperial" is a headline, not a footnote. Every touchpoint — the site, the social, the inbox — should lead with the institutions that already believe you.

Lever · Positioning
02

The university network is a ready-made pilot.

Cambridge, Michigan, Brown, Dartmouth, Imperial — that's not a logo wall, it's a network we can activate. Start with a pilot inside the schools that already trust you, then decide together what scaling into new markets looks like.

Lever · Network
03

Each channel is a different lever — not just "more posts."

The current social footprint is a starting line, not a verdict. Instagram & TikTok are where the student and early-researcher half of your audience already lives — visibility, demos, day-in-the-life. LinkedIn is where the decision-makers read — deans, librarians, institutional buyers. Different platforms, different jobs, one calendar.

Lever · Channels
04

Four audiences, one thin funnel.

Students, researchers, professionals and institutions each need a different proof point. Right now they're being served the same way. Sharper segmentation = sharper conversion at every stage.

Lever · Funnel
05

21 days free + 50% student discount is a gift, half-unwrapped.

A three-week trial with full access and a 50% education discount is rare in this category — and almost invisible on your social. Every post for the student audience should land on that offer. It's the cheapest, fastest conversion lever you own.

Lever · Offer
06

Teams & Enterprise is a different sale — and a different story.

Plus sells to individuals on TikTok. Teams & Enterprise — SSO, private AI, library integrations, centralized billing — sells to deans, librarians and IT on LinkedIn and via direct outreach. Two engines, not one bigger megaphone.

Lever · Tiers
03 · How I'd help

Four workstreams,
one calm operating system.

I run all of this hands-on, no agency layer. You get one person who writes, ships, measures and adjusts — anchored to the network you already have.

Brand & story

Sharpen the narrative so a researcher, a student and a dean each hear what they need in the first three seconds. Your design is already strong — this is about what it says, not how it looks.

  • Messaging house & audience pillars
  • Audience-specific copy (students · researchers · deans)
  • One-pagers per audience

Content & social engine

A weekly rhythm on LinkedIn, X and TikTok that turns the product's depth into stories people actually share. Editorial first, demos baked in.

  • Weekly content calendar & production
  • Short-form video: "watch me research"
  • Newsletter for researchers & deans
Pilot

Network pilot & activation

Start where the trust already lives — a pilot inside the universities that already vouch for Wonders (Cambridge, Michigan, Brown, Dartmouth, Imperial). Prove the playbook there, then we define together what scaling into new markets looks like. Here is how it could shape up:

  • Campus ambassadors inside your existing network
  • Co-built case studies with the schools who already use you
  • Founder voice & press, paced to the milestones we set together

Conversion & growth tracking

Every piece of content has a destination. Funnels mapped from impression to signup, dashboards we both look at weekly, and the discipline to kill what doesn't work.

  • UTMs, attribution & lifecycle emails
  • Landing pages per audience
  • Weekly growth review
04 · Creative I'd capture

The shots I want to film,
the threads I want to write.

Production can be in-house, UGC from students & ambassadors, or a mix — whatever fits your current rhythm. We'll calibrate to your team.

North star: show a stressed student's "before" become a calm "after."

▶ Series · vertical video

"Watch me do a literature review in 7 minutes."

Why it works · The product's superpower is speed. Sped-up screen captures with a real researcher's voiceover do more in 30 seconds than any landing page ever will.

★ Documentary · campus

"I used to dread the first week of a new paper. Now I open Wonders and I'm already three steps in."

— PhD candidate · interview series

Why it works · Real researchers, on camera, in their own words. Trust scales when peers vouch — universities follow students faster than the other way around.

⌗ Founder-led · short-form

Repurpose the CEO's YouTube tutorials into short-form gold.

↳ Reels · TikTok · LinkedIn-native video

Why it works · The CEO is already a natural storyteller — the YouTube tutorials prove it. That voice is the most under-leveraged asset Wonders owns. We cut, reframe and pace them for vertical feeds — same person, ten times the reach, zero extra filming days.

⚡ Myth-busting

"Will it hallucinate citations?"

→ Direct answer + receipts.

"Will my professor know?"

→ Show the workflow. Reframe the fear.

Why it works · Every researcher has the same three fears. Answer them in public and you remove the last objection between curiosity and signup.

✦ Micro-education

60-second explainers: how to read a paper, faster.

Skimming Citations Note-taking

Why it works · Teaches before it sells. The tool becomes the obvious next step in a habit we already built for the viewer.

05 · The plan

An ongoing partnership,
truly integrated with your team.

No fixed sprint, no clock running out. I'd integrate with however your team likes to work — embedded, async, weekly syncs, your tools — in stages, not deadlines. We check in at every milestone and adjust together.

01 Where we start

Foundation

Listen first. Audit the funnel, sharpen the story, stand up the engine, get embedded in your tools and rituals — so everything that comes next is about reach, not setup.

  • Audience & positioning audit
  • Messaging house & audience-specific copy
  • Content calendar & tooling stood up
  • Tracking, UTMs, dashboards
02 Once we're moving

Momentum

Ship in public. Weekly content rhythm, first testimonial captures on real campuses, and the founder voice finds its frequency.

  • Steady weekly content rhythm, multiple formats
  • Student testimonials filmed on real campuses
  • Newsletter launched
  • First paid pilots inside the existing network
03 Ongoing, together

Pilot & scale — shape together

The pilot pays off in the network we already have. From there, we'd sit down and decide — together — what scaling into new markets actually looks like for Wonders. Here is one shape it could take:

  • Ambassadors live across schools already in your network
  • Case studies + press paced to milestones we set together
  • Lifecycle emails & funnel tuned per audience
  • Roadmap for new-market expansion, co-decided
06 · Why me

I do for brands what Wonders does for research.

"

I take something complex and make it feel simple, clear, and enjoyable — the same job Wonders does for the people it serves.

A few years back, I was the one drowning in tabs — writing a Master's thesis at LUISS, Rome (Innovation & Sustainability). Highlighting PDFs at 2am, losing citations in the wrong folder, talking to myself in the library. When I came across Wonders, the thought was very specific: where were you when I needed you? This is part of why I'm writing this — not from a deck, from the other side of the screen.

Agnès Dézard
Marketing Consultant
Sw

Swaven (acquired by MikMak)

Social · demos · case studies

Joined while it was scaling fast — owned the content engine through Statista's "Top 500 Champions of Growth" recognition, and stayed on through the MikMak acquisition. Translated a complex martech product into stories executives actually shared.

★ Acquired by MikMak · Statista Top 500
Pp

PassPass

Social from zero · ambassador program

Built the social presence from a standing start and ran the ambassador program end-to-end. The same playbook — finding real people on campus, equipping them, paying them in something they care about — is exactly what a network pilot for Wonders would lean on.

★ Direct precedent for a campus ambassador pilot
Mu

Muro Art (acquired by Sedition)

Editorial · interviews · website

Long-form artist interviews, blog editorial, and website copy through Muro's fast-growth stretch — right up to its acquisition by Sedition. Proof I can carry a brand voice that reads like a person, not a content farm. Researchers respond to exactly that.

★ Acquired by Sedition · editorial voice
